assemblykor - Handover (2026-03-11)

Source: HANDOVER_2026_03_11.md

Project Overview

assemblykor is an R package providing curated Korean National Assembly datasets for teaching quantitative methods in political science. Korean politics counterpart to palmerpenguins.

  • Version: 0.1.0
  • Repository: https://github.com/kyusik-yang/assemblykor
  • License: MIT (code), data licenses vary (see README)
  • Local path: /Volumes/kyusik-ssd/kyusik-research/projects/assemblykor/

Package Contents

7 Built-in Datasets

Dataset Records Scope
legislators 947 20-22nd assembly legislator metadata
bills 60,925 20-22nd assembly bill metadata
wealth 2,928 Asset disclosure panel (13 time points)
seminars 5,962 Policy seminar activity panel
speeches 15,843 22nd assembly Sci & ICT committee transcripts
votes 7,997 Plenary votes (20-22nd)
roll_calls 368,210 22nd individual legislator votes

7 Exported Functions

  1. get_bill_texts() - Download bill rationale texts (60,925, ~40MB parquet)
  2. get_proposers() - Download co-sponsorship records (769,773, ~25MB parquet)
  3. list_tutorials() - List available tutorials
  4. run_tutorial(name) - Run learnr interactive tutorial
  5. open_tutorial(name) - Copy Rmd to working directory
  6. path_to_file(file) - Get CSV file path
  7. set_ko_font() - Auto-set Korean font for ggplot2

9 Tutorials (2 formats each)

# Topic Key Methods Datasets
1 R Basics and tidyverse dplyr core functions, pipe, join legislators, bills, wealth
2 Data Visualization with ggplot2 bar, histogram, boxplot, scatter, line, facet legislators, bills, wealth, seminars
3 Regression Analysis OLS, multiple regression, log transform, interaction, coefficient plot legislators, wealth, seminars
4 Panel Data and Fixed Effects Pooled OLS vs FE, two-way FE, DiD, cluster SE wealth, seminars
5 Introduction to Text Analysis keyword frequency, TF-IDF, committee comparison, speech analysis bills, speeches
6 Co-sponsorship Network Analysis network construction, centrality, community detection, cross-party legislators, bills
7 Roll Call Analysis Rice Index, rebel votes, voting heatmap, agreement matrix legislators, votes, roll_calls
8 Bill Success Factors binary outcome, logistic regression, odds ratio, predicted probability bills, legislators
9 Speech Patterns Analysis speech frequency/length, turn structure, agenda keywords, diversity speeches

Two formats: - inst/tutorials/ - learnr interactive (browser-based, English section headings) - inst/rmd-tutorials/ - plain Rmd (RStudio editing, Korean headings)

Changes This Session (2026-03-11)

New Tutorials (07-09)

Created 3 new learnr tutorials:

  • 07-roll-call-analysis: Covers votes/roll_calls data, Rice Index calculation, rebel vote identification, voting heatmap, agreement matrix. Each exercise includes full data prep for learnr isolation. 3 exercises with hints.
  • 08-bill-success: Binary outcome creation, OLS vs logistic comparison, glm(family=binomial), odds ratios, predicted probabilities, AIC/pseudo-R2 comparison. Quiz blocks for binary variables and logistic concepts. 3 exercises.
  • 09-speech-patterns: Role analysis, speech frequency/length, turn structure, agenda keyword dictionaries (AI, Semiconductor, Broadcasting, Telecom, Privacy), member-agenda heatmap, diversity measurement. 3 exercises.

Synced Tutorials (01-06)

All 6 existing learnr tutorials fully rewritten to match enhanced rmd-tutorial content:

  • 01: Added variable explanation table, comparison operators table, detailed pipe explanation, arrange section, bills/wealth data exploration, join types table. 5 exercises.
  • 02: Added density comparison (Gangnam property), chart selection guide, polishing step-by-step section with party colors, ggplot2 grammar explanation, quiz for position=“fill”. 4 exercises.
  • 03: Added OLS formula explanation, coefficient plot with broom::tidy(), model diagnostics table, log transformation explanation, results summary table. Pre-built merged dataset. 4 exercises.
  • 04: Added detailed between/within explanation, OVB concept, demeaning explanation, switchers concept, time-invariant limitation, cluster SE. Uses fixest::feols() and etable(). 3 exercises.
  • 05: Added speeches data analysis (role-based TF-IDF), AI keyword tracking in speeches, temporal comparison (20th vs 22nd), expanded stopwords (46 terms), preprocessing flow diagram, tokenization limitation note. 3 exercises.
  • 06: Added network concept visual (5-node example), centrality comparison scatter plot, cross-party heatmap, detailed modularity explanation, 21st vs 22nd assembly comparison exercise. 3 exercises with hints.

Learnr Format Consistency

All 9 tutorials now follow the same pattern: - English section headings (Korean data content preserved) - set_ko_font() instead of manual font detection code - CSS injection via htmltools::includeCSS(system.file(...)) - tutorial_options(exercise.eval = FALSE) - Each exercise chunk self-contained (includes its own data prep) - Korean Unicode escapes for string literals in learnr context

Technical Notes

  • CSS theming: Custom dark theme (652 lines), Catppuccin Mocha inspired. Fonts: Inter (UI), JetBrains Mono (code). Uses htmltools::includeCSS() in each tutorial Rmd.
  • Data pipeline: data-raw/prepare_data.R (R) + process_*.py (Python). Raw source files (.gitignored): speeches_all_committees.csv (3.1GB), speeches_full.parquet (1.4GB), roll_calls_raw.csv (44MB).
  • Dependencies: arrow required only for download functions. Core package depends only on base R + utils.
  • LazyData: Compressed with xz. Package install includes all 7 .rda files.
  • Arrow ALTVEC fix: All 7 .rda files re-saved with forced as.character() conversion to prevent serialization errors from arrow’s ALTVEC strings.

Known Data Characteristics

  • n_bills in legislators: includes co-sponsorship. Median 702, max 4,198.
  • n_bills_lead: lead proposals only. Median 56, max 696.
  • 661 unique legislators across 947 rows (some serve multiple assemblies).
  • wealth unit: thousands of KRW (divide by 1,000,000 for billions).
  • roll_calls API only available for 22nd assembly.
  • speeches: 22nd assembly Sci & ICT committee only (15,843 records).
  • Party colors: PPP (#E61E2B), DPK (#004EA2), RRP (#00B0F0).

Next Steps
